title: Smažený sýr
subtitle: Czech breaded fried cheese
excerpt: A thick slab of semi-hard cheese, breaded and fried until the coating is golden and the cheese inside has melted into a pulling, savoury mass. The Czech answer to fast food — served with tartare sauce and chips.
sourceNotes: Smažený sýr (fried cheese) is one of the most widely eaten dishes in the Czech Republic, found on the menu of every Czech pub and restaurant. Traditionally made with Edam cheese (eidamský sýr), a mild semi-hard yellow cheese that melts well without becoming runny, the dish is also made with Hermelín (a Czech Camembert-style cheese). This version uses a firm semi-hard cheese in place of the traditional Edam, which is not always available outside Central Europe. The breading method is the standard Czech style: flour, beaten egg, breadcrumbs, fried in oil.

recipe.freezeNotes: Does not freeze once breaded and cooked.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bread the cheese and refrigerate for up to 2 hours before frying. Cold breading holds better in the oil.

content.text: The only challenge in smažený sýr is preventing the cheese from leaking through the breading during frying. A double coat of egg and breadcrumbs is the solution: flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs, then egg again, then breadcrumbs again. This double coating creates a robust shell that holds together in the hot oil and contains the melted cheese inside.
The cheese must be refrigerator-cold when it goes into the oil. Warm cheese begins melting before the coating sets, and can leak. Bread it, then refrigerate for 30 minutes before frying.

items.prepNote: cut into 2 slices, each about 1.5 cm thick

content.text: Season the {{plain-flour}} with {{sea-salt-fine}}. Set up three shallow dishes: flour, beaten {{eggs}}, and {{breadcrumbs-dried}}. Coat each piece of {{gruyere}} in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs. Dip in egg again and coat with a second layer of breadcrumbs, pressing firmly to adhere. Place on a plate and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

Heat the {{sunflower-oil}} to 175°C in a frying pan with at least 2 cm of depth. Lower the breaded cheese into the oil carefully. Fry for 2 to 3 minutes until deep golden on the first side, then turn and fry for 2 minutes on the second side. Drain on kitchen paper. Serve immediately with {{mayonnaise}} and chips or bread.

items.cause: Single coat instead of double, or cheese too warm
items.fix: Always use a double coat of egg and breadcrumbs. Ensure the cheese is cold (straight from the fridge) before frying and do not fry at too high a temperature

items.cause: Not pressing the breadcrumbs on firmly enough
items.fix: After applying each coating, press firmly with the palm of your hand to compact the breadcrumbs. The double coat should feel solid before it goes in the oil
Reduce to 170°C. The coating should turn golden over 2 to 3 minutes per side — slow enough for the cheese inside to melt before the exterior burns

content.text: Smažený sýr is on every Czech pub menu. It is the dish that Czech vegetarians rely on when everything else on the menu contains meat, and it is the dish that Czech children choose when they are taken out to eat. It is served with chips (hranolky) and tartare sauce (tatarská omáčka) in the pub context, and with bread at home. The golden, crisp exterior concealing the melted cheese inside is one of those combinations that requires no improvement or elaboration.
